name: OpenClaw
tagline: The personal AI assistant you actually own
description: A self-hosted assistant that runs on your own devices, with a local gateway bridging AI models to the places you already talk: WhatsApp, Telegram, Slack, Discord, Signal, iMessage. Voice, a live canvas UI, multi-agent routing, and companion apps for macOS, iOS, and Android.
why: One of the fastest-growing repositories in GitHub history, and the poster child for the personal-agent movement: full assistant infrastructure — channels, memory, device control — under MIT, rather than a hosted subscription.
useCases: An always-on personal assistant reachable from your existing chat apps
A self-hosted alternative to hosted assistant products
audience: Tinkerers, self-hosters, and developers who want an assistant they can open up

setup.requirements: A Node-based gateway on an always-on machine (Mac, Linux, or VPS), API keys or local models, and per-channel credentials

limitations: The attack surface is broad: an internet-reachable gateway wired to your messaging accounts and shell is a prompt-injection target. Run it least-privilege, review channel and tool permissions, and expect fast-moving docs.
take: The clearest signal yet that the assistant wars will be fought partly on your own hardware. Extraordinary momentum — bring your own security discipline.
